widgets_values:
TLDR
Implemenation in comfyui based on project here: https://github.com/ID-LoRA/ID-LoRA

You Need:
ID Lora - from site above
Reference: First frame image (or video with num frames div by 8 + 1, ie 9, 17 frames)
Reference: Audio clip with 3-5s of audio, make sure you set the sample rate of the clip to match the source.

Prompt formatted into the expected format


Custom nodes
IDLoRAGuider - audio and video conditioning to anchor output to reference image/audio
IDLoRAPrepareVideo - sort of like img to video, but treats the source more like an anchor with a look-back feature of the lora
IDLoRAPromptFormatter - help format the prompt properly to what the model wants
IDLoRAAudioNoiseInjector - ensure the ltxvemptyaudiolatent output is suitable for audio 
IDLoRAPrepareAudioReference - take an audio sample input and output conditioning for generation

Guider CFG values
CFG - overall strength 2-5
ID_CFG - strength to reproduce the likeness 2-5
AUDIO_CFG - strength to reproduce the pitch/tone of the audio sample

Model locations

Diffusion, vae(s), text projection clip: 
  https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy
Upscale:
  https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/tree/main
Gemma heretic:
  https://huggingface.co/DreamFast/gemma-3-12b-it-heretic/tree/main/comfyui

lora optimizer is optional, but trained loras do step on each other, so I recommend it

using no distill on the high denoise lowres pass let's the id-lora do its thing without interference 

using distill on the low denoise highres pass on the other hand really helps to add those final details, like skin.

This node will format the prompt into the expected structure

[VISUAL]: <scene and appearance description>
[SPEECH]: <exact words the person should say>
[SOUNDS]: <speaker vocal style + ambient/environmental sounds>

* {id_tag} [woman] or [jenny]

* {subject 1 description} [woman] with long blonde hair and green eyes.
* optional {subject 2 description} [man] or [kevin] with short dark hair and a trimmed beard

* {scene} [woman] nodding slowly while looking at the camera

* {speech} [woman] "it's a pleasant day"

* {background sounds} birds chirping in the distance, a piano playing softly

* {negative} low quality, blurry, static

## TAG
> [Linda]
## Person1 description
> woman, long wavy blonde hair, tall, slender, fair skin, deep raspy voice
## Person2 description
> [man] stout, olive skin tone, middle-aged, deep baritone voice
## Scene
> an amateur scene unfolding outdoors against a mountain backdrop, a cool wind shakes the trees and kicks up fallen leaves from the ground.  
A white stag in the distance standing majestic, illuminated by a ray of sun
[Linda] stands in the foreground looking at the stag holding black binoculars over her eyes.
[Linda] speaks with a tone of wonder and excitement
[man] stands next to her nodding approvingly
## speech
> [Linda] "would you look at that?"
## sounds
> wind howling
## negative
> low quality, robotic tone, music, piano

widgets_values: euler and dpmpp_2m seem best for adding detail, but in some scenes dpmpp_2m seems to go too far, so making euler the defailt

low res
downscale by 2 while ensuring resulting dimensions are divisible by 32 (min 512)
Bypass repeat latent batch for video input

high res
low res * 2 match upsampling
preseed the latents with the last frame
